fixture.description: New crypto codec converts String<->byte[] via getBytes()/new String() with no Charset, silently using the platform default — non-ASCII secrets corrupt across hosts/locales. Base64 (ASCII) is NOT the defect.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that `plain.getBytes()` and/or `new String(dec)` omit an explicit Charset and therefore use the JVM platform default, so a secret encrypted on one host/locale (UTF-8) can fail to round-trip on another (ISO-8859-1/Windows-1252) and any non-ASCII byte is corrupted; the fix is StandardCharsets.UTF_8 on both conversions. Acceptable severity: IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. FAIL if the missing-charset issue is not surfaced. The report should NOT claim the Base64 encode/decode is the bug (Base64 output is ASCII and correct).

expected_behavior: Bugs/security agent flags the charset-less getBytes()/new String() as platform-default-dependent
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT claim Base64 encoding is the defect
